steps.title: Spot the contrast between achieved success and unproven claim
steps.content: NASA successfully altered a secondary object's orbit, yet scientists have yet to demonstrate the same works for primary objects. The opposition between this achievement and the lack of proof is the decisive signal. The transition must capture this contrast.

steps.title: Match the transition signal to the choices
steps.content:
Though signals the needed contrast: even though secondary objects were successfully altered, scientists still haven't demonstrated the same for primary objects.

The other three choices fail to create this opposition—one introduces an example, one suggests a similarity not yet proven, and one simply adds information.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
